title: teucrium capitatum subvar. caespitulosum
intro: Teucrium capitatum subvar. caespitulosum, also known as the Creeping Germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium capitatum plant, a type of evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The Creeping Germander has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Teucrium capitatum subvar. caespitulosum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
